source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: These models don't have data to be trained on… human learning is a big data learning process… ImageNet + neural network + GPU was the golden recipe.
core_belief: Capability gaps are often data gaps before architecture gaps.
operational_pattern: Invest in curated benchmarkable datasets that force the field to measure progress on hard perceptual tasks.
organizational_archetype: Research lab or frontier AI company on scaling laws
hidden_assumptions: Label quality and taxonomy matter as much as volume
contradictions: Scale-more-data vs need for world models
AI optimism vs societal risk
behavioral_signals: Researchers obsess over architecture while ignoring data
Companies avoid AI label until market flips
emotional_political_dynamics: Frustration when society assigns breakthroughs to lone heroes
operational_adaptations: Pair scaling with domain-specific datasets where LLMs plateau
ai_era_implications: Data supply chains remain strategic; world models may be next bottleneck after text

source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: AI is a civilization-level technology… we need a guiding framework anchored in human benevolence.
core_belief: Technology outcomes are chosen by people, not predetermined by models.
operational_pattern: Institutionalize policy bootcamps, public reporting (AI Index), and legislative engagement alongside research.
organizational_archetype: University-linked institute or platform company with public trust exposure
contradictions: Move fast on capabilities vs slow governance
Optimism about net benefit vs preventing societal screw-ups
behavioral_signals: Teams ship without workforce impact analysis
AI for good without internal review loops
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of blame for harms not individually caused
operational_adaptations: Embed human-impact review in consumer AI launch criteria
ai_era_implications: PMs need societal-impact fluency; evals include harm and labor dimensions

pattern_name: World models after language scaling ceiling
source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: LLMs can't count chairs in video like a toddler… need world models for spatial intelligence; scaling alone insufficient.
core_belief: Language scaling hits perceptual/spatial ceilings; next innovations are multimodal world models.
operational_pattern: Invest in spatial AI while maintaining human-centered policy; don't conflate AGI marketing with science.
organizational_archetype: Research lab plus startup spinning world models
hidden_assumptions: World model data collectible at scale
failure_modes: Ignoring spatial evals on chat-only roadmaps
contradictions: ChatGPT as thinking machine vs incomplete spatial reasoning
emotional_political_dynamics: Scientist frustration at AGI definitional chaos
operational_adaptations: Product teams add visual/spatial eval suites
ai_era_implications: PMs in physical-world products should track world-model stack not only LLM APIs

source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: Industry underestimates societal pushback on AI; humanity dictates adoption more than technology capability.
core_belief: Deployment is bounded by human comfort and adoption curves, not lab capability alone.
operational_pattern: Prioritize human-centered adoption design; expect pushback; align product with societal acceptance.
organizational_archetype: Consumer platform with long human-interaction thesis
contradictions: Internal AI enthusiasm vs consumer social realism
behavioral_signals: AR anchored in world not HUD notifications
operational_adaptations: Agent workflows anchored to real jobs-to-be-done
ai_era_implications: Societal adoption becomes primary gating function for consumer AI features

pattern_name: North star scientific questions over hype cycles
source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: We need to keep asking audacious scientific questions… not just chase the next benchmark.
core_belief: Enduring labs are sustained by question quality, not only leaderboard position.
operational_pattern: Protect long-horizon research portfolios against quarterly hype pivots; publish benchmarks that redirect field.
hidden_assumptions: Talent motivated by mission not only comp
contradictions: Publish benchmarks while warning against benchmark obsession
behavioral_signals: Repeated investment in new dataset categories
Researchers cite mission not only SOTA
operational_adaptations: Dual track: capability research plus HAI policy
ai_era_implications: Eval culture in products mirrors scientific benchmark discipline

pattern_name: Jobs change by task bundle not overnight elimination
source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: We should think about tasks and skills… jobs are bundles of tasks that will be reorganized.
core_belief: Displacement happens at task level first; policy and training should target task portfolios.
operational_pattern: Map occupations by automatable tasks; invest in human skills that complement models in same workflow.
failure_modes: Treating AGI timeline as workforce plan
contradictions: Optimism about net jobs vs near-term displacement pain
emotional_political_dynamics: Hope vs fear in public narrative
operational_adaptations: AI Index tracks labor indicators not only capability
ai_era_implications: HR and PM role design starts from task graphs not job titles

source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: Spatial intelligence is the next frontier… language-only scaling will not get us to embodied intelligence.
core_belief: Physical-world AI requires different data, evals, and talent than text labs optimize for.
operational_pattern: Fund spatial/world-model bets in parallel with LLM scaling; build evals for geometry, physics, navigation.
contradictions: Text products fund company while claiming spatial is next
behavioral_signals: Spatial eval failures on otherwise SOTA models
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of another AI winter if bets fail
operational_adaptations: Consumer products add visual reasoning eval gates
ai_era_implications: Multimodal evals become PM requirement for any camera/AR/robotics feature

source_episode: The Godmother of AI on jobs, robots & why world models are next | Dr. Fei-Fei Li
source_quote_or_context: You cannot do responsible AI as an afterthought… it has to be parallel to capability building.
core_belief: Safety and capability built sequentially guarantees public trust collapse at scale.
operational_pattern: Run policy education, red-teaming, and societal impact review alongside model training milestones.
hidden_assumptions: Safety org has teeth not only PR
failure_modes: Safety team vetoed without escalation path
contradictions: Ship fast culture vs parallel safety work
operational_adaptations: HAI-style bootcamps for product leaders
ai_era_implications: Launch criteria include societal impact like security review

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: Product Hunt product of the day/week/month… signups spiked then flattened… we didn't have strong word of mouth.
core_belief: PMF is proven by organic pull, not launch-day trophies.
operational_pattern: After vanity spikes plateau, all-hands rebuild onboarding until users evangelize without paid spend.
organizational_archetype: Lean prosumer AI startup in crowded incumbent category
hidden_assumptions: Team survives 3–4 months rework on low runway
AI delivers magic in first session
failure_modes: Brute-forcing top-of-funnel while core loop weak
contradictions: Growth marketing vs product-led word of mouth
behavioral_signals: Flat signup curve after launch spike
operational_adaptations: In distribution-heavy categories design growth from day one
ai_era_implications: AI onboarding magic can unlock PMF if first-session output is shareable

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: Users are selfish, vain, and lazy… what can you give in the first 30 seconds that earns the next 30 seconds.
core_belief: Attention is rented in 30-second increments; onboarding is the product.
operational_pattern: Concentrate limited eng bandwidth on door-to-door experience not feature breadth (restaurant metaphor).
hidden_assumptions: AI generation reliable in first session
failure_modes: Beautiful core with broken first mile
contradictions: Onboarding team vs core product false split
operational_adaptations: Pull most magical capability to second zero of session
ai_era_implications: AI belongs in onboarding path not bolted on later

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: All initial influencers I onboarded manually… jump on a call… tell your story in their voice… thousands of micro influencers.
core_belief: Authentic distribution is unscalable at the start and that is the moat.
operational_pattern: CEO-led creator onboarding: walkthrough, hook brainstorming, light feedback—never scripted ads to million-follower accounts.
organizational_archetype: Founder-led marketing at efficient AI startup
pressure_scenarios: Creator churn after one paid post
contradictions: Founder-led marketing vs hire growth team early
behavioral_signals: One-off creator posts with no repeat
operational_adaptations: Onboard influencers like customers; expect repeat posts from invested relationships
ai_era_implications: AI multi-model orchestration is downstream; distribution still human-trust-limited

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: Not one model—20+ models, orchestration, routing cheaper vs frontier… laser-focused workflow.
core_belief: Wrapper is viable when you own deep workflow and model portfolio economics.
operational_pattern: Monetize early when users demand credits; anchor pricing to ChatGPT familiarity; monitor unit economics monthly.
failure_modes: Chasing shiny use cases without decade commitment
contradictions: Stay small/profitable vs blitzscale
behavioral_signals: Intercom: how do I buy credits
Million ARR within months of monetization
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of being labeled just a wrapper
operational_adaptations: Pick problem worth decade before scaling ads
ai_era_implications: PMs must understand model routing cost and eval per step

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: Idea in morning, prototype, recruit users with zero skin in the game, ship by afternoon, watch struggle, fix by evening.
core_belief: Learning velocity beats planning cycles when runway is tight.
operational_pattern: Daily micro-experiments with unaffiliated users; observe struggle live; iterate next day.
organizational_archetype: Pre-PMF startup with design founder sensibility
hidden_assumptions: Scope small enough for same-day ship
contradictions: Same-day loop vs enterprise 6–12 month deploy
operational_adaptations: Pair with 30-second onboarding focus for maximum signal
ai_era_implications: AI prototypes cheap; discipline shifts to who you watch in session

pattern_name: One egg not five at onboarding
source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: Scott Belsky one egg… we put all our eggs in making the first 30 seconds magical.
core_belief: Concentrated onboarding beats scattered feature excellence pre-PMF.
operational_pattern: Reject spreading eng across five onboarding bets; single magical path to shareable output.
hidden_assumptions: Team agrees on the one egg
failure_modes: No share loop at end of egg
contradictions: Later portfolio expansion requires many surfaces
operational_adaptations: Revisit multi-egg only after WOM coefficient positive
ai_era_implications: Cheap generation increases temptation to ship five eggs—discipline matters more

source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: We made a bet-the-company decision: make the first 30 seconds magical… three or four months of rework.
core_belief: When organic fails, fixing activation is existential not incremental.
operational_pattern: Pause feature roadmap; redirect all eng to activation until referral and return curves inflect.
organizational_archetype: Sub-scale ARR prosumer company with low runway
failure_modes: Giving up before iteration count sufficient
contradictions: Feature shipping culture vs onboarding tunnel
behavioral_signals: Zero paid spend while fixing product
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of running out of runway
operational_adaptations: Define exit criteria for tunnel before entering
ai_era_implications: AI can shorten build time but not remove need for observed user struggle

pattern_name: Revenue per employee as discipline metric
source_episode: "Dumbest idea I've heard" to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee
source_quote_or_context: We stayed lean… very high revenue per employee… profitability gave us freedom to invest in brand.
core_belief: Efficiency is strategic optionality, not only investor aesthetics.
operational_pattern: Resist headcount goals that distort hiring quality; monetize when users pull for credits; track inference cost monthly.
hidden_assumptions: Lean team can maintain prod quality
contradictions: Brand investment vs stay scrappy
operational_adaptations: Hire only when bottleneck is clearly human
ai_era_implications: Inference line item becomes core finance metric for AI apps

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Founder led sales is not about revenue on day one. It is about learning as fast as humanly possible.
core_belief: Early commercial work is market research conducted by the highest-authority person.
operational_pattern: Founder runs sales to harvest budding moments that reshape positioning; defer revenue hire until ~$1M ARR.
organizational_archetype: Zero-to-one B2B without brand or references
hidden_assumptions: Vision novel enough to spark curiosity
contradictions: Sell vs learn
Founder vision vs market reality
behavioral_signals: Salesperson blamed when vision is off
Prospects say cool but don't multi-thread
emotional_political_dynamics: Founder ego protection via blaming sales
operational_adaptations: 30 highly crafted outreaches before Clay volume
ai_era_implications: AI startups need paid services to teach buyers process before SaaS attach

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Stay away from better… shock value or counterintuitive… 3–4 sentences, no scroll on mobile.
core_belief: Response rate is primarily insight quality, not channel tactics.
operational_pattern: Email/LinkedIn: role relevancy, counterintuitive hook, problem focus, mobile brevity—leave them wanting more.
hidden_assumptions: Problem felt in market (2% vs 12% interest)
pressure_scenarios: Domain health ruined by blast campaigns
contradictions: Personalization industry vs relevancy-first
operational_adaptations: Track win rate not just meetings
ai_era_implications: AI outbound without insight increases noise

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: When you're vulnerable… not fully built yet… easier to tell someone before you make this mistake.
core_belief: Buyers protect founders' feelings when they think product is done; honesty requires plausible incompleteness.
operational_pattern: First call: state early stage, passion for problem, request how problem manifests—no demo-first; book second call on first call.
contradictions: April Dunford educate-market later vs Jen learn-first early
emotional_political_dynamics: Founder fear of no problem here
operational_adaptations: Gate: is problem measured? How tried to solve? Book next meeting live
ai_era_implications: AI demos dazzle while hiding workflow fit—delay demo until problem validated

pattern_name: Sell services to educate the budget
source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: 40–50% have to sell some form of service before technology… paid to educate.
core_belief: When buyers lack process for novel problem, services revenue is GTM not failure.
operational_pattern: Co-author scope; sell education/process design; time-box; parallel tech plus service contracts.
hidden_assumptions: Services budget larger than innovation budget
pressure_scenarios: 18-month wait if you don't own education
failure_modes: Competitor educates account while you wait
contradictions: SaaS metrics vs services wedge
behavioral_signals: Buyers ask HITL and metrics questions
operational_adaptations: Truncate services; onboard users before tech gate
ai_era_implications: PMs spec HITL and evals in services SOW—they become product requirements

pattern_name: Sell the alpha not the feature
source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Sell the alpha, not the feature… they're buying into where you're going and the problem you're solving.
core_belief: Enterprise buyers at $1M–$10M ARR fund asymmetric insight, not feature parity.
operational_pattern: Lead with counter-narrative and problem thesis; defer feature catalog until buyer confirms pain is strategic.
contradictions: Product maturity needed eventually despite alpha sell
behavioral_signals: Buyer asks for vision not RFP checklist
emotional_political_dynamics: Buyer fear of career risk on novel vendor
operational_adaptations: Vision narrative tied to buyer's measured pain
ai_era_implications: AI alpha is workflow transformation not model benchmark

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Low conversion is okay if win rate is high… more at-bats vs better messaging.
core_belief: Meetings without win rate signal are vanity; high win rate with low volume is positioning problem not effort problem.
operational_pattern: Track win rate by segment; if win rate high, increase targeted at-bats; if low, fix insight/ICP before scaling outbound.
hidden_assumptions: Enough opportunities to measure win rate
contradictions: Volume culture vs Jen's craft outbound
operational_adaptations: 30-email test before hiring SDR
ai_era_implications: AI can write emails but not counter-narrative—that stays founder-owned

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Vanta market-first accelerated PMF; Figma/Airtable technical insight uncapped upside but riskier.
core_belief: Starting from market pain speeds fit but may cap outcome; technical insight inverts risk/reward.
operational_pattern: Choose consciously: interview-driven pain vs vision-led search; sales insight quality mirrors path.
hidden_assumptions: Investors tolerate search for tech bets
failure_modes: Never finding market on tech-led bet
contradictions: Accelerated PMF vs blitzscale outcome
emotional_political_dynamics: Investor how huge is market fights
operational_adaptations: If tech-led, founder sales discovers market via budding moments
ai_era_implications: AI wrappers need market pain clarity; foundation models favor tech-led with distribution risk

source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
source_quote_or_context: Procurement shuts down if too novel… you have to educate legal, risk, success metrics before tech.
core_belief: Novel AI purchases fail in legal/procurement unless process education is budgeted and paid.
operational_pattern: Simple language; clearly different from incumbents; parallel service contract to teach HITL, evals, risk framework.
contradictions: Move fast AI vs enterprise change management
operational_adaptations: Time-box services with explicit tech migration triggers
ai_era_implications: Compliance and evals belong in first SOW not v2 product

pattern_name: Column B vision with chaos-to-clarity ladder
source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: Column B: dream the magical future… ladder to the moon with rungs… chaos to clarity: write it down, pitch deck, designs, prototype.
core_belief: Breakthroughs require improbable future backcast into embarrassing first steps.
operational_pattern: 10-year vision → mission pillars → crazy big goals → incremental clarity steps per project.
organizational_archetype: Mission-driven product company at global scale
pressure_scenarios: Daily ops pull to Column A
contradictions: Crazy big goals vs reliable delivery dates
behavioral_signals: No vision deck for new initiatives
operational_adaptations: Per-project chaos→clarity pipeline
ai_era_implications: AI features must ladder to Column B mission not hype bolt-ons

source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: 100+ investors said no… add slide on market size, competitive gap, problem-first pages.
core_belief: Investor rejection is structured feedback if converted into deck mutations.
operational_pattern: Each objection → new slide; problem before solution; find tribe that gets it.
contradictions: Persist vs find new tribe
behavioral_signals: Pitch starts with solution not problem
emotional_political_dynamics: Frustration vs investors wrong but helpful
operational_adaptations: Fundraising as chaos→clarity like product
ai_era_implications: AI pitch decks need problem/society slides not model features

source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: Front-end rewrite estimated six months, took two years—no product shipped… product company not shipping is not fun.
core_belief: Platform rewrites are multi-year morale bets; shipping identity must be gamified to survive.
operational_pattern: Bath-toy board game standups; accept CBG slippage while protecting platform foundations.
contradictions: Ship weekly vs platform pause
operational_adaptations: Only enter tunnel with clear unlock (collaboration, i18n)
ai_era_implications: AI velocity makes multi-year freezes harder—modularize so AI ships on branches

pattern_name: Ugly baby protection for fragile ideas
source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: New ideas are like ugly babies… leadership has to protect them until they can stand on their own.
core_belief: Early ideas are socially vulnerable; premature critique kills nonlinear bets.
operational_pattern: Separate ideation forums from execution critique; celebrate CBGs even when rungs look embarrassing.
contradictions: Protect ideas vs ship fast culture
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame on small first steps vs huge mission
operational_adaptations: Vision deck before company-wide critique
ai_era_implications: Cheap prototypes increase ugly babies—need faster kill criteria too

pattern_name: Crazy big goals as motivation device
source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: Crazy Big Goals… feel inadequate before them… that's motivating not demoralizing.
core_belief: Goals should embarrass you slightly or they aren't directing enough energy.
operational_pattern: Set public CBGs with celebration at milestones; pair with rungs so inadequacy converts to action.
contradictions: CBG ambition vs investor quarterly cadence
operational_adaptations: CBGs at company level; OKRs at execution level
ai_era_implications: AI compresses rungs—CBGs may need shorter horizons

source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: Empower the world to design… design shouldn't be gatekept by experts with expensive tools.
core_belief: Long-horizon mission justifies multi-year infra bets that don't optimize quarterly metrics.
operational_pattern: Align roadmap to democratization thesis; measure success by non-designer output quality not only ARR.
contradictions: Democratization vs pro/designer segment revenue
behavioral_signals: Pro tier exists but mission stays broad
operational_adaptations: AI features framed as empowerment not replacement
ai_era_implications: AI lowers design skill floor—mission validated but moat shifts to distribution and brand

source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: We pitched 100+ times… find investors who get the vision, not lean startup purists for Canva.
core_belief: Wrong investor tribe wastes years; right tribe funds unconventional paths.
operational_pattern: Iterate deck for objections but don't change Column B vision for wrong audience; seek design-believers.
contradictions: Persist vs pivot debate
behavioral_signals: Same vision different deck over years
Investor meetings cluster by thesis fit
operational_adaptations: Track investor archetype not only yes/no
ai_era_implications: AI companies face same tribe problem—technical vs GTM investors

pattern_name: Operational chaos to clarity on every bet
source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
source_quote_or_context: Chaos to clarity on every project… write it down, pitch deck, designs, prototype—don't keep it in your head.
core_belief: Unwritten ideas create alignment illusions; clarity artifacts scale beyond founder intuition.
operational_pattern: Mandatory vision deck per initiative before resourcing; prototype as alignment not only sales.
contradictions: Clarity process vs move fast
operational_adaptations: Lighter deck for small bets; full for CBG-level
ai_era_implications: AI can generate decks faster—human must still own Column B thesis

pattern_name: GM to functional for AI depth
source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Went from GM structure to functional org… singular focus to drive AI and platform depth.
core_belief: You ship your org structure; portfolio of mini-companies blocks platform transformation.
operational_pattern: Reorg: all eng→one leader, all design→one leader; painful change; Conway's Law as visceral lesson.
hidden_assumptions: Exec team models daily AI use
pressure_scenarios: AI hype vs pilot failure narrative
contradictions: Autonomous BUs vs platform leverage
operational_adaptations: Leaders use Goose on real workflows first
ai_era_implications: AI-native requires functional platform plus MCP orchestration

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: AI cut tests 50%; deleting bad tests saved 2–3x more… question base assumptions.
core_belief: Portfolio of optimizations beats silver-bullet AI; ask if process should exist.
operational_pattern: Automate Block priority; measure 8–10 hrs/week saved; delete tests and cloud offload before AI-select tests.
contradictions: AI 10x narrative vs 20–25% hours saved today
behavioral_signals: Risk/legal builds shadow IT with Goose
operational_adaptations: Question vendor feature—build MCP overnight
ai_era_implications: Greatest productivity may be fewer asks via self-serve not faster code

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Goose gives LLMs arms and legs via MCPs… tries alternate routes on failure.
core_belief: Enterprise AI leverage is integrability and retry-rich autonomy not chat alone.
operational_pattern: Open-source agent; Snowflake/Tableau reports; experimental screen listener on core team.
pressure_scenarios: Ping-pong 5-minute sessions vs hours autonomy goal
failure_modes: Building highway fills with more cars
contradictions: Open source vs competitive differentiation
emotional_political_dynamics: Excitement vs creepiness of always-on agent
operational_adaptations: Allow agent route flexibility and retry
ai_era_implications: MCP orchestration layer may be where enterprises win

pattern_name: Exec daily use before change management
source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Jack and exec team use agents daily… you can't mandate what you don't model.
core_belief: AI transformation fails when leadership reads LinkedIn instead of using agents on real work.
operational_pattern: Require executives to automate personal workflows first; then org-wide mandate has credibility.
organizational_archetype: Public company C-suite driving AI-native shift
pressure_scenarios: Employee cynicism from mandates without tools
contradictions: Top-down mandate vs bottom-up discovery
emotional_political_dynamics: Hypocrisy resentment if leaders don't use tools
operational_adaptations: Executive AI office hours
Personal workflow wins before KPIs
ai_era_implications: Change management becomes show-your-agent-screen

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Sometimes the answer is delete the process… AI on a bad process just makes bad things faster.
core_belief: Automation without process audit multiplies waste.
operational_pattern: Before AI project: ask should this workflow exist; kill meetings, tests, handoffs; then automate remainder.
hidden_assumptions: Political will to kill sacred cows
failure_modes: AI on top of bureaucratic process
contradictions: AI-first mandate vs delete-first discipline
behavioral_signals: Test count drops before AI test selection
emotional_political_dynamics: Middle managers protect headcount via process
operational_adaptations: Process deletion OKR before automation OKR
ai_era_implications: Agents make deleting-and-rebuilding cheaper—bias toward rebuild

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Biggest surprise was non-technical teams… weeks to hours on marketing reports and workflows.
core_belief: Highest ROI often outside engineering—where manual work was worst.
operational_pattern: Prioritize Goose/MCP for marketing, ops, support before forcing eng-only pilots.
contradictions: Engineering-led AI strategy vs business-led wins
operational_adaptations: Vertical MCP packs per function
ai_era_implications: Citizen builder wave is enterprise AI's hidden adoption curve

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: When you build a bigger highway you get more traffic… efficiency creates more demand.
core_belief: Productivity gains increase appetite for work unless scope is governed.
operational_pattern: Pair efficiency programs with prioritization discipline; expect demand surge after AI unblocks.
hidden_assumptions: Leadership can say no after efficiency
contradictions: Do more with less rhetoric vs sustainable load
operational_adaptations: Capacity planning post-AI
WIP limits
ai_era_implications: AI removes excuse of capacity—prioritization becomes binding constraint

source_episode: How Block is becoming the most AI-native enterprise in the world | Dhanji R. Prasanna
source_quote_or_context: Know your company DNA… ours is engineering and design first—we reorganized to match that.
core_belief: Org structure must reinforce actual strategic DNA not holding-company convenience.
operational_pattern: Diagnose DNA before copying Netflix/Spotify models; align functional structure to how you win.
organizational_archetype: Company transforming from conglomerate to platform
failure_modes: DNA rhetoric without hiring/ promotion alignment
contradictions: Platform narrative vs historical BU autonomy
operational_adaptations: Platform KPIs in reviews
ai_era_implications: AI-native DNA requires integrated data—holding co model fails

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: People think: latest AI news, agentic framework, vector DB… Actually: talk to users, reliable platform, better data, workflows, prompts.
core_belief: App quality is won on data, workflows, and reliability—not toolchain novelty.
operational_pattern: Prioritize user research, workflow optimization, RAG data prep over framework churn.
organizational_archetype: AI app team in enterprise or startup
contradictions: Move fast vs invest in platform
behavioral_signals: Team reads AI news more than tickets
operational_adaptations: Publish what actually improves apps checklist for leadership
ai_era_implications: PM skill: workflow mapping and data contracts

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: Exec: 80%→85% for two engineers vs new feature… pick battles… catastrophic domains need tyrannical evals.
core_belief: Evals are insurance proportional to downside and competitive advantage.
operational_pattern: Stepwise eval on agent pipelines; skip fixed count—cover failure modes that matter.
organizational_archetype: Scaling AI product with executive feature appetite
hidden_assumptions: Can estimate incremental lift from eval investment
contradictions: Vibe shipping vs eval culture
behavioral_signals: Engineers propose 2 eng-months for 5% lift on side feature
emotional_political_dynamics: Practitioner joy in eval craft vs budget scrutiny
operational_adaptations: Deep research: eval each fan-out search step
ai_era_implications: PMs own outcome metrics; evals align to user-valued segments

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: Who should write eval? Not separate—system problem across components and user behaviors.
core_belief: Evaluation is product instrumentation for non-deterministic systems.
operational_pattern: Break eng/product wall; align evals to user care-abouts; senior review on AI-generated code.
failure_modes: PM throws eval over wall to ML
contradictions: Functional titles vs blurred AI roles
behavioral_signals: No owner for end-to-end agent quality
operational_adaptations: Start with prod segments failing
ai_era_implications: Headcount may shrink at junior layer; eval plus review become factory floor

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: Most gains come from better data and prompts before swapping to the newest model.
core_belief: Model churn is seductive; data and workflow fixes are unglamorous and high leverage.
operational_pattern: Invest in RAG corpus quality, labeling, and prompt iteration before architecture changes.
failure_modes: Endless model A/B without data fix
contradictions: Build for next model vs fix today
behavioral_signals: Quality jumps after data clean not model swap
High spend on models flat metrics
operational_adaptations: Data prep sprint before model upgrade
ai_era_implications: Model routing matters after data baseline solid

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: The hard part is not the demo—it's reliable, maintainable systems in production.
core_belief: Demo-success creates false confidence; production is different optimization problem.
operational_pattern: Treat prod requirements (latency, cost, failure modes, monitoring) as day-one not phase two.
contradictions: Move fast research preview vs enterprise reliability
operational_adaptations: Prod checklist parallel to demo
ai_era_implications: Observability and evals are the new on-call stack for AI

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: Many agent frameworks are scaffolding that will be absorbed by models and platforms.
core_belief: Heavy framework bets have short half-life in fast-moving model era.
operational_pattern: Keep harness thin; own workflow and data; avoid deep lock-in to orchestration libraries.
organizational_archetype: Startup or enterprise AI platform team
contradictions: Teach frameworks in courses while warning in prod
operational_adaptations: Interface boundaries around framework-specific code
ai_era_implications: Moats are workflow data and distribution not orchestration glue

pattern_name: User research still first on AI apps
source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: Talk to users—still the first thing that actually improves AI applications.
core_belief: Non-determinism doesn't remove need for grounded user problems.
operational_pattern: Qualitative and quantitative user research before eval investment on wrong feature.
failure_modes: Building AI features users don't want
contradictions: Move fast without research
behavioral_signals: Low usage despite high eval scores
operational_adaptations: Weekly user touchpoints on AI features
ai_era_implications: AI can simulate users but not replace grounded discovery

source_episode: AI Engineering 101 with Chip Huyen (Nvidia, Stanford, Netflix)
source_quote_or_context: You have to think about cost and latency as design constraints, not afterthoughts.
core_belief: LLM products fail unit economics when cost is bolted on after architecture freeze.
operational_pattern: Model routing, caching, batching, and smaller models for easy steps designed into v1.
contradictions: Subsidize tokens early vs cost discipline
operational_adaptations: Cost dashboard per feature
Haiku for easy steps
ai_era_implications: PM owns cost-per-successful-user metric

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: Four metrics: lead time, deploy frequency, MTTR, change fail rate… speed and stability move in tandem.
core_belief: Fast and stable delivery is achievable with capabilities not slogans.
operational_pattern: Measure four keys; benchmark elite; improve capabilities per Accelerate research.
contradictions: Elite targets vs legacy release trains
behavioral_signals: Rising fail rate with deploy push
operational_adaptations: Pair DORA with SPACE for local metrics
ai_era_implications: AI codegen speed is not productivity—review-heavy workflow needs new metrics

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: SPACE: Satisfaction, Performance, Activity, Communication, Efficiency/flow—use at least three dimensions.
core_belief: Complex creative work requires balanced intentional metrics—not one vanity number.
operational_pattern: After DORA diagnostic pick context-fit metrics across SPACE; triangulate surveys with telemetry.
contradictions: DORA fixed four vs SPACE pick-your-own
operational_adaptations: Write problem statement in a week; hunt any signal
ai_era_implications: Add trust/reliance dimension for AI tools

pattern_name: AI productivity is not halving headcount
source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: HTTP server 50% faster isn't productivity… enables harder work… changed mental model, friction, cognitive load.
core_belief: Copilot-class tools shift work from writing to reviewing; workforce cuts from micro-benchmarks are category error.
operational_pattern: Study holistic outcomes; communicate balanced metrics to CEOs; prepare SPACE evolution for trust/reliability.
contradictions: Experts love AI vs fear replacement
operational_adaptations: Define problem before buying DevEx tools
ai_era_implications: PMs plan review-heavy SDLC; juniors need mentors not only autocomplete

pattern_name: Surveys beat instrumentation when they conflict
source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: When surveys disagree with advanced instrumentation, surveys are almost always correct.
core_belief: Felt experience captures friction telemetry misses.
operational_pattern: Triangulate DevEx surveys with system metrics; act on survey when conflict.
failure_modes: Ignoring survey because data says fine
contradictions: Objective data religion vs qualitative signal
behavioral_signals: Developers cite flow breaks instrumentation misses
operational_adaptations: Week-one problem statement plus any signal hunt
ai_era_implications: Measure review burden and trust in AI output

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: Measurement journey: more subjective early, more systemic later—you need both.
core_belief: Mature measurement builds from interviews and surveys toward system metrics—not only dashboards day one.
operational_pattern: Phase 1: interviews and surveys define problem; Phase 2: instrument systems; Phase 3: correlate and refine.
organizational_archetype: Org starting DevEx or AI productivity program
pressure_scenarios: Board wants AI ROI number in 30 days
contradictions: Google-scale methods at startup scale
behavioral_signals: Can't answer what problem we're solving
operational_adaptations: Google-style phases adapted to company size
ai_era_implications: AI tool ROI requires phased methodology not single metric

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: Never use lines of code—it's the worst metric for knowledge work.
core_belief: Activity metrics that reward volume destroy quality and incentives.
operational_pattern: Ban LOC/PR count as performance metrics; use outcomes, flow, and satisfaction.
organizational_archetype: Any engineering org under AI pressure
contradictions: Need some activity signal for capacity planning
behavioral_signals: Review time explodes while LOC up
emotional_political_dynamics: Middle managers default to visible activity
operational_adaptations: Educate execs on review-time metric
ai_era_implications: AI makes LOC even more misleading

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: Write down the problem you're trying to solve in a week—then hunt for signals.
core_belief: Tool-first AI productivity programs fail without diagnosed bottleneck.
operational_pattern: One-week problem definition workshop before buying copilot/enterprise AI seats.
organizational_archetype: Enterprise rolling out AI coding tools
contradictions: Move fast adopt AI vs diagnose first
operational_adaptations: Baseline DORA/SPACE before rollout
ai_era_implications: AI tool evals should include developer trust not only speed

source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
source_quote_or_context: Frictionless is about capabilities and practices that reduce friction—not declaring less process.
core_belief: Sustainable speed comes from technical and organizational capabilities documented in Accelerate.
operational_pattern: Invest in CI, architecture, psychological safety, and small batches—not only AI licenses.
hidden_assumptions: Not using frictionless as excuse to cut needed process
contradictions: AI replaces process narrative vs capabilities book
operational_adaptations: Map initiatives to DORA/SPACE drivers
ai_era_implications: AI amplifies existing friction if capabilities weak

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: Core search needs unchanged… AI is expansionary—more questions, Lens +70% YoY… not death of search.
core_belief: AI grows total query space; does not zero-sum replace navigational search.
operational_pattern: Ship AI Overviews plus Lens multimodal plus AI Mode with fan-out grounding in Shopping/Maps/Web.
organizational_archetype: Incumbent consumer platform with research plus product fusion
failure_modes: Copying chat UX without intent fit
contradictions: Keywords vs natural language
behavioral_signals: Users paste hard questions into Google
operational_adaptations: Integrate previews in SERP then deep link AI Mode
ai_era_implications: SEO becomes intent satisfaction for AI fan-out queries

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: AI Mode does query fan-out—dozens of searches, data backends, paired with content satisfying rater intent guidelines.
core_belief: Helpful AI answers are search problems multiplied not single-prompt completions.
operational_pattern: Construct via many sub-queries plus live data graphs; rank sources by enduring quality guidelines.
organizational_archetype: Incumbent search plus frontier model partnership
contradictions: Chat UX simplicity vs backend complexity
behavioral_signals: Sites with intent match in overviews
operational_adaptations: Publishers: design for intent satisfaction
ai_era_implications: AEO: optimize for selection in fan-out research passes

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: Close Friends failed: favorites mistranslated, one person added, loop not closed… 2–3 years to find where it worked.
core_belief: Consumer wins require obsessive problem decomposition not feature cloning.
operational_pattern: Ship broad → measure where works → simplify scope → rename → design for 20–30 friend loop.
hidden_assumptions: Leadership tolerates years on strategic bets
contradictions: Speed culture vs multi-year bake
operational_adaptations: JTBD for causation
Simplify until loop closes
ai_era_implications: AI features need same loop analytics

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: There's a massive long tail of questions people already ask Google that AI unlocks.
core_belief: Incumbent distribution wins on queries users already attempt but get poor answers.
operational_pattern: Optimize for hard multi-sentence and multimodal queries; don't only chase head terms.
contradictions: Expansionary AI vs protect core search revenue
operational_adaptations: Separate metrics for exploratory vs navigational
ai_era_implications: Long tail explodes with natural language—ops cost is the constraint

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: We have distribution and data graphs startups don't… that compounds over time.
core_belief: In platform shifts, distribution plus grounding data often beats feature-first startups long-term.
operational_pattern: Integrate AI into existing high-intent surfaces; leverage Shopping/Maps/Lens graphs.
contradictions: Innovator's dilemma on ad model
behavioral_signals: AI features in core SERP not only labs
operational_adaptations: Ship in core product not only beta site
ai_era_implications: Startups need data moats—not only better model

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: We've used quality raters for years… what satisfies intent is surprisingly stable guidance.
core_belief: Human rater frameworks encode durable intent satisfaction better than viral AI demos.
operational_pattern: Align AI Overviews/Mode to rater guidelines; use raters when metrics conflict with intuition.
contradictions: Move fast vs quality bar
operational_adaptations: Publish rater-informed SEO guidance
ai_era_implications: LLM evals should encode intent satisfaction not only fluency

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: Competing Against Luck… understand the job to be done not just the feature request.
core_belief: Feature failures are often wrong job hypotheses not execution failures.
operational_pattern: When feature underperforms, run JTBD analysis before killing or scaling.
contradictions: Data-heavy Google vs qualitative JTBD
operational_adaptations: Close Friends playbook: segment then simplify
ai_era_implications: AI features need job hypothesis not prompt hypothesis

source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
source_quote_or_context: There was real urgency… but we also had to be thoughtful about search's role in the ecosystem.
core_belief: Incumbent turnaround requires focused urgency plus ecosystem stewardship.
operational_pattern: Accelerate AI shipping while managing publisher, ads, and quality stakeholder tradeoffs.
hidden_assumptions: Quality org has veto power when needed
contradictions: Startup speed vs incumbent responsibility
operational_adaptations: AI Mode as distinct surface for experimentation
ai_era_implications: Platform companies need ecosystem evals not only user evals

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: Headlines vs ground: dig roads for broadband… enterprise robustness 6–12 months… 95% POC fail is denominator/hype.
core_belief: Production AI is change-management and judgment-encoding not demo magic.
operational_pattern: Expert labeling plus RL environments; legal/policy approval; resist clickbait POC failure rates.
organizational_archetype: Enterprise buyer plus data partner plus frontier lab
contradictions: Move fast AI vs 6–12 month hardening
operational_adaptations: Count only serious deployments in success metrics
ai_era_implications: PM roadmap: compliance eval HITL—not feature demos

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: 80% bachelor's+, 15% PhD… trajectory recordings, Salesforce agent environments… generalizable tasks worth more.
core_belief: As models improve, data must be expert, environment-rich, and generalizable.
operational_pattern: Shift from cheap generalist labeling to PhD-heavy tasks; build RL sandboxes.
organizational_archetype: Frontier lab supplier plus enterprise solutions
contradictions: Scale pioneered cheap labeling vs now expert-heavy
behavioral_signals: Labs request fixes Scale surfaced first
operational_adaptations: Two businesses: data to labs, solutions to enterprises
ai_era_implications: Human expert work shifts to environment design and eval authorship

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: Keep very wide aperture on ideas… mobile convenience trucks in DC—crickets—needed cigarettes/beer traffic drivers.
core_belief: Bad-at-start ideas deserve dig time; ground truth beats headlines.
operational_pattern: Explore many bets until unit economics and traffic drivers clarify; humility from failure string.
contradictions: Explore vs exploit at scale
behavioral_signals: Orders of magnitude miss on demand tests
operational_adaptations: Small geo probes before national scale
ai_era_implications: Keep aperture wide on use cases until environment-data loop proves

pattern_name: Most enterprise data is useless for training
source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: Most companies have petabytes that aren't useful for training… need curated expert judgment.
core_belief: Raw enterprise volume ≠ training moat; curation and task design determine value.
operational_pattern: Audit data for generalizable tasks; invest in expert labeling and environments not lake size.
organizational_archetype: Enterprise AI buyer and data vendor
pressure_scenarios: Board says we have the data
contradictions: Data scale marketing vs expert quality reality
operational_adaptations: Start with task-specific datasets
ai_era_implications: Eval environments become the unit of data value

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: We serve frontier labs with data and enterprises with solutions—different sales cycles, same core capability.
core_belief: Lab and enterprise buyers need different packaging of same underlying data expertise.
operational_pattern: Separate GTM and product for lab research velocity vs enterprise robustness; shared expert network.
failure_modes: Enterprise sales culture on lab buyers
contradictions: Focus vs diversification
operational_adaptations: Clear metrics per business line
ai_era_implications: Data/eval vendors must pick buyer archetype or bifurcate org

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: When you're the operator you hear when things go wrong—that keeps you grounded.
core_belief: Customer-facing operators develop skepticism that protects against hype narratives.
operational_pattern: Leadership spends time on failed POCs and deployment blockers not only sizzle reels.
contradictions: Optimistic lab partnerships vs cynical enterprise reality
operational_adaptations: Rotate execs through customer support escalations
ai_era_implications: AI leaders need deployment-time not demo-time exposure

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: 95% of POCs fail—easy POCs that shouldn't have started inflate the denominator.
core_belief: Reported enterprise AI failure rates mix serious deployments with vanity science projects.
operational_pattern: Filter POC pipeline; only count resourced deployments with executive sponsor and production path.
contradictions: High fail rate narrative vs real production wins
operational_adaptations: POC gate checklist before resources
ai_era_implications: Serious deployments need eval + compliance in POC scope

source_episode: First interview with Scale AI's CEO: $14B Meta deal, what's working in enterprise AI, and what frontier labs are building next | Jason Droege
source_quote_or_context: Enterprise AI is about encoding expert judgment into systems that work in their environment.
core_belief: Value is operationalizing expertise not model access alone.
operational_pattern: Build environment-specific agents with expert-in-the-loop data; measure robustness not demo accuracy.
contradictions: Scale speed vs enterprise thoroughness
emotional_political_dynamics: Experts as high-value workers not cost center
operational_adaptations: Price on outcomes and robustness not seats
ai_era_implications: Mercor/Surge model: experts write evals that encode judgment

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: Macro explore/exploit is common; I work at insight level… game review cold patterns → +25% reviews, +20% subs.
core_belief: Growth teams die by local optimization; wins must be amplified across surfaces before brainstorming anew.
operational_pattern: Oscillate: divergent experiments → articulate insight → adjacent PMs exploit → detect saturation → re-explore.
contradictions: Brand big swings vs experimentation compounding
emotional_political_dynamics: Excitement on negative results with lesson
operational_adaptations: Experiment explorer tools plus AI Slack bots for patterns
ai_era_implications: AI accelerates explore; exploit still needs prod bridge and stats

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: User retention is gold… without retention, onus on day-one pay… Duolingo/Chess organic WOM.
core_belief: Word-of-mouth plus retention beats paid desperation for subscription consumer.
operational_pattern: Target high experiment volume; focus existing-user retention for daily habit products.
contradictions: Free reach vs monetization
emotional_political_dynamics: Indie paywall dream vs treadmill reality
operational_adaptations: Grow market size not only share
ai_era_implications: AI must deepen habit loops not novelty

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: Game review taught worst moves… flip to celebrate best moves—+25% game reviews, +20% subscriptions.
core_belief: Reframing feedback from deficit to celebration unlocks engagement in learning products.
operational_pattern: Audit cold/defeat patterns in UX; run exploit wave on positive reinforcement.
hidden_assumptions: Users avoid pain of seeing mistakes
contradictions: Brutal honesty pedagogy vs positive psychology
emotional_political_dynamics: Pride in intellectual rigor resists softening
operational_adaptations: Document hypothesis for swarm
Treat huge losses as valuable
ai_era_implications: LLM coaches need evals for tone/outcome not only correctness

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: When you find something that works, exploit it across the product before exploring again.
core_belief: Insight half-life is short; cross-surface propagation captures value before saturation.
operational_pattern: Run exploit playbook across modules (copy, color, success rating) until p-values cluster insignificant.
pressure_scenarios: AI hype pushing random LLM features
contradictions: Explore novelty vs exploit discipline
behavioral_signals: Same insight in puzzles onboarding email
operational_adaptations: Leader mandates insight broadcast
ai_era_implications: AI generates variants faster—exploit phase needs human insight anchor

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: Duolingo aspired to 1000 experiments a year… we did ~250 at Chess—volume matters.
core_belief: High experiment throughput increases odds of non-obvious insights.
operational_pattern: Invest in platform, stats, and PM literacy; celebrate learning from losses; leader reviews cross-portfolio patterns.
contradictions: 250 vs 1000—scale-dependent
operational_adaptations: Right-size volume to team; keep insight process
ai_era_implications: AI drafts hypotheses; humans own design and interpretation

pattern_name: Grow the market not only share
source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: Duolingo grew the language-learning market… chess grew interest in chess during Queen's Gambit.
core_belief: Category growth beats zero-sum share fights for habit products.
operational_pattern: Invest in brand, content, community when tailwind hits; capture with onboarding and loops.
contradictions: Brand marketing vs growth team science
emotional_political_dynamics: Community passion vs growth gimmick tension
operational_adaptations: Spike playbook pre-built
ai_era_implications: AI content can create micro-spikes; retention still determines value

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: Chess engines vs LLM… use the right technology for the user value, not hype.
core_belief: Specialized AI often beats general LLM for domain-specific user outcomes.
operational_pattern: Evaluate whether LLM is best tool; prefer proven domain AI when quality bar is competitive.
hidden_assumptions: Users care about quality not tech
contradictions: AI everywhere mandate vs domain excellence
behavioral_signals: LLM features underperform engine features in tests
operational_adaptations: LLM for language layers; engines for analysis
ai_era_implications: Product teams need AI tool selection discipline not default LLM

source_episode: How to find hidden growth opportunities in your product | Albert Cheng (Duolingo, Grammarly, Chess.com)
source_quote_or_context: My job is often to look across experiments and see patterns others miss.
core_belief: Individual PMs optimize locally; growth leader compounds by connecting insights across surfaces.
operational_pattern: Weekly portfolio review; experiment explorer; require insight docs on wins and large losses.
organizational_archetype: Head of growth at multi-PM growth org
failure_modes: Leader only reacts to biggest win
contradictions: Democratized experimentation vs central pattern role
operational_adaptations: Insight doc template mandatory on |Δ|>threshold
ai_era_implications: AI surfaces correlations; leader validates causality
